# Agent setup

Install the Codex-compatible wk skill so agents can run the same local wiki protocol as the CLI.


Categories: `agent`, `setup`, `instructions`

Aliases: `codex setup`, `wk skill`

Files: `skills/wk/SKILL.md`, `skills/wk/agents/openai.yaml`, `docs/setup.md`, `src/cli/commands/installAgent.ts`, `src/cli/commands/setup.ts`

For Codex-compatible IDEs, run `wk setup --profile mixed --audience all --agent codex` when initializing a repo. To refresh only the companion skill later, run `wk install-agent codex` or `wk install-agent codex --yes` after previewing the destination.

The installed `/wk` skill gives agents the repo protocol for `status`, `spin`, records, render/site, closeout, and Beads coordination. It treats structured records as the source of truth and keeps generated Markdown and static-site files deterministic.

A bare `wk`, `/wk`, `$wk`, or linked `$wk` invocation is intentionally lightweight: if `wiki-site/index.html` exists, the agent should return one concise local link to that generated site instead of running status/spin, starting a server, explaining the skill, or modifying files.

For CLI updates, install `@thjodann/wk@latest` through npm or the repo package manager, then rerun `./node_modules/.bin/wk install-agent codex --yes` to refresh the installed skill from the packaged source.
